assumptions: All components run in a single Go process using goroutines and channels.
The task queue is an in-memory channel-based queue (no external broker).
The data store is an in-memory map protected by a sync.RWMutex.
The system handles at most 1000 concurrent tasks.
constraints: No external dependencies (no databases, message brokers, or cloud services).
All components must be testable with standard Go testing tools.
The system must handle graceful shutdown on SIGINT/SIGTERM.
success_criteria: API gateway accepts tasks via HTTP POST and enqueues them.
Task processor dequeues tasks, processes them, and stores results.
All components shut down cleanly within 5 seconds of receiving SIGTERM.
No data loss on graceful shutdown (in-flight tasks are completed).

test_plan.strategy: unit tests with go test and integration tests using test helpers

test_cases.name: TestAPIEnqueue
test_cases.description: Test that API gateway correctly enqueues a task

test_cases.name: TestProcessorDequeue
test_cases.description: Test that task processor dequeues and processes tasks

test_cases.name: TestGracefulShutdown
test_cases.description: Test that system shuts down gracefully without data loss

test_cases.name: TestConcurrentTasks
test_cases.description: Test that system handles 100 concurrent tasks without race conditions
